title: Metastability and clock-domain-crossing risk
description: A flip-flop sampled near a clock edge can go metastable, so asynchronous crossings need synchronizers to bound the failure rate.
body: Use when reasoning about why CDC needs special handling. Do not let STA time an asynchronous crossing as synchronous, because the launch and capture clocks have no fixed relationship. Actions: identify each crossing, ensure a multi-flop synchronizer or handshake bounds the metastability window, and apply asynchronous constraints. Validation signals include every crossing covered by a synchronizer and a CDC report free of unsynchronized crossings. Risk boundary: metastability failures are rare and intermittent, so treat any unsynchronized crossing as a latent field failure.
